entity_accounting.purpose: Explains the current relationship between registered tables, frontend entities, readiness state, and field coverage after canonical publication.

schema_tables.description: Logical runtime-contract tables currently governed by the canonical table registry.

schema_authority.description: Migrations/schema.sql own storage truth. The frontend/runtime registry targets logical contract tables; partition children are storage implementation details.

frontend_entities.description: Frontend entity registrations, including non-table views and additional aliases over the same physical table where business presentation differs.

readiness.description: Current readiness counts are taken directly from frontend-foundation-catalog.json and reflect the live publication state, not historical onboarding assumptions.

field_coverage.derivation: 764 registry tables -> 764 tables referenced by data-fields artifacts -> 0 tables without field definitions.

why_counts_differ.775_vs_764: 8 non-table entities and 3 additional table-view aliases explain why frontend entity registrations exceed physical tables.
why_counts_differ.764_vs_764: Field coverage is now full across the canonical table registry.
why_counts_differ.conclusion: Count differences are governed and explainable. Any remaining delta must map either to a non-table entity or a deliberate alias over an existing physical table.
